content.problemStatement:
In the only movie theatre in Chefland, there is a single long row of MMM seats. The seats are indexed 111 to MMM from left to right. There is exactly one ticket for each individual seat. NNN people, numbered 111 to NNN, want to buy tickets for seats in this row.

Person iii wants exactly SiS_iSi​ number of tickets. They also have a strong preference for viewing angles. Specifically, person iii requires that each of their seats must be indexed between AiA_iAi​ and BiB_iBi​. It is guaranteed that Ai≥Ai−1A_i \ge A_{i-1}Ai​≥Ai−1​ for 2≤i≤N2 \le i \le N2≤i≤N.

For each iii (1≤i≤N1 \le i \le N1≤i≤N), calculate the largest jjj (i≤j≤Ni ≤ j ≤ Ni≤j≤N) such that all people from iii to jjj can be accommodated together and given tickets according to their requirements.


Input Format

The first line contains TTT - the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first line of each test case contains two integers NNN and MMM - the number of people and seats respectively.
NNN lines then follow. The iii-th line contains integers AiA_iAi​, BiB_iBi​, and SiS_iSi​ - the acceptable seat indices for person iii and the required number of tickets.


Output Format
For each test case, output a single line with NNN space-separated integers. The iii-th of these integers should be the largest valid jjj (i≤j≤Ni \leq j \leq Ni≤j≤N) such that all people from iii to jjj can be accommodated together.
constraints: Sum of NNN over all test cases is at most 5⋅1055 \cdot 10^55⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111:

i=1i = 1i=1: It can be shown that person 222 cannot be accommodated with person 111. The maximum valid jjj in this case is 111 itself.
i=2i = 2i=2: We can accommodate persons 222 and 333 together, and more people after person 333 cannot be accommodated. One way to do this is by ensuring that the set of seats assigned to person 222 is {3,4,6,7,8}\{3, 4, 6, 7, 8 \}{3,4,6,7,8} and to person 333 is {5}\{5 \}{5}.
i=3i = 3i=3: We can accommodate persons 333 and 444 together, and more people after person 444 cannot be accommodated. One way to do this is by ensuring that the set of seats assigned to person 333 is {4}\{4 \}{4} and to person 444 is {5,6,7,8,9}\{ 5, 6, 7, 8, 9 \}{5,6,7,8,9}.
i=4i = 4i=4: We cannot accommodate more people after person 444.
i=5i = 5i=5: We can accommodate all persons 555, 666, and 777 together. One way to do this is by ensuring that the set of seats assigned to person 555 is {6}\{6 \}{6}, to person 666 is {7}\{ 7 \}{7}, and to person 777 is {8}\{ 8 \}{8}.
i=6i = 6i=6: We can accommodate persons 666 and 777 together. One way to do this is by ensuring that the set of seats assigned to person 666 is {7}\{ 7 \}{7} and to person 777 is {8}\{ 8 \}{8}.
i=7i = 7i=7: The maximum jjj possible is 777 itself.
